title: Git branch documentation
finding: git branch --show-current prints the current branch name, which is not the same as proving HEAD resolves to a commit.

title: Git glossary - unborn branch
finding: Git HEAD can point at a branch that does not yet exist and has no commit; Git calls this an unborn branch.

title: Git rev-parse documentation
finding: git rev-parse --verify HEAD is the documented way to verify that HEAD names a valid object, and it errors when the revision is not valid.

title: Git checkout documentation
finding: git checkout -b creates a new branch from an optional start point. The start point must resolve to an object Git can use.

title: GitHub REST API - repository contents
finding: The create-or-update file endpoint accepts a commit message and Base64 file content, which supports the existing --auto-init-repository README initialization path.
